name: Incline Dumbbell Press
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to 30–45°. Sit with dumbbells on your thighs. Kick the dumbbells up one at a time as you lie back, holding them at shoulder width above your chest. Lower the dumbbells to chest level, keeping your elbows at about 45° from your torso. Press the dumbbells back up to full arm extension, squeezing your upper chest.
form_cues: Keep your shoulders pinned back and down
Do not bounce at the bottom
safety_notes: Use a spotter for heavy sets. If you feel anterior shoulder pain, lower the incline angle or reduce depth. Do not lock your elbows violently.
common_mistakes: Using too much weight and losing range of motion
